Arshad, thanks for the updated patch.  A couple issues still remain.  Will you push a new version, or is it back in my court?

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1
> 
> (4 comments)
> 
> Arshad, thanks for the updated patch.  A couple issues still remain.  Will you push a new version, or is it back in my court?

Andreas, I will take care of this. 

>(defect?) Also, it seems like *both* of these checks cannot be true at the same time, so one or the other will always be printed?  So it looks like the single regexp above is *required* and not just a simplification.

I remembered seeing 'rc' sometimes as %ld and %d so I updated for both. Yes this is a defect, both cannot be true at same time. Will fix this with just %d as your original.

Patch Set 3:

> >(defect?) Also, it seems like *both* of these checks cannot be
 > > true at the same time, so one or the other will always be printed? 
 > > So it looks like the single regexp above is *required* and not just
 > > a simplification.
 > 
 > I remembered seeing 'rc' sometimes as %ld and %d so I updated for
 > both. Yes this is a defect, both cannot be true at same time. Will
 > fix this with just %d as your original.

My proposed regexp "%l?d" will match both "%d" and "%ld" so that would be preferred.

Patch Set 4:

Verified +1

Here is my test line. They all pass as expected.

CERROR("[Testing020] Testing\n"); /* checkpatch shows both %s and rc = %d warn */
CERROR("%s: [Testing0200] Testing\n", "Lustre"); /* checkpatch shows only rc = %d warn */
CERROR("[Testing02000] Testing: rc = %d\n", 0); /* checkpatch shows only %s warn */
CERROR("[Testing02000] Testing: rc = %ld\n", 0); /* checkpatch shows only %s warn */
CERROR("%s: [Testing020000] Testing: rc = %lld\n", "lustre", 0); /* checkpatch shows only rc = %d warn */
CERROR("%s: [Testing020000] Testing: rc = %d\n", "lustre", 0); /* no %s or rc = %d warn */
CERROR("%s: [Testing020000] Testing: rc = %ld\n", "lustre", 0); /* no %s or rc = %d warn */

Patch Set 7:

I don't know about changing RETURN/GOTO with end of switch. This change is ignoring something upstream doesn't like.

Patch Set 7:

> I don't know about changing RETURN/GOTO with end of switch. This
 > change is ignoring something upstream doesn't like.

I'm not sure what you mean?  It already allows "return" and "goto" as end-of-case, just not the RETURN/GOTO macros. The only thing I changed was to make the check case insensitive.

parents.subject: LU-15404 ldiskfs: use per-filesystem workqueues to avoid deadlocks

commit.subject: LU-16634 build: improve checkpatch warnings
commit.message:
LU-16634 build: improve checkpatch warnings

Change checkpatch.pl to allow RETURN/GOTO as "end of switch case".

Improve CERROR/CWARN/LCONSOLE/CDEBUG message checking/warning to
print more useful message style advice than just "think hard".

Allow "DFID|DOSTID" within long error strings without complaint.

Add a spelling.txt rule to warn if version checks are added in a
test for future versions.  This is mostly useful for maintenance
branches when patches are being backported with test cases.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I1a0d2f839949debf346aa15c65b0f407e0ce7057
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/50331
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Arshad Hussain <arshad.hussain@aeoncomputing.com>
Reviewed-by: Neil Brown <neilb@suse.de>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
